title: schiedea globosa
intro: Schiedea globosa, also known as the Mauna Kea gilia, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the island of Hawaii. This plant belongs to the borage family and is characterized by its pink or white flowers and blue-green leaves. The medicinal properties of Schiedea globosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas or infusions, to promote overall health and well-being, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function.
